change_summary: finding shrunk -31% (178→122c, c2 dup-fix applied) and detail_verdict_text expanded +893% (98→973c) since c2 fingerprint on 2026-04-07; b/a wandering point integrated. Cross-reference and structural review needed.
our_argument_summary: Misleading verdict on the elliptical disc geometry claim: (1) adding free parameters always reduces residuals — AIC/BIC required; (2) the 48.6% RMS reduction is unverifiable (no script in the repo computes it, paralleling the 95.2% accuracy figure); (3) the inter-city distances being fitted are WGS84-derived, so the ellipse is absorbing azimuthal-equidistant projection distortion rather than discovering a physical disc shape; (4) b/a wanders 0.70–0.90 across V5–V9 (29% drift) vs WGS84 b/a = 0.997, revealing an adjustable fitting parameter rather than a measured geometry; (5) the dome's OPEN-003 ('ellipse parameters still converging') is a self-acknowledged escape clause.

relevant_files: test_curve_stretching.py (ellipse test uses sqrt(1-(r/R)²) — semicircle, not true ellipse)
find_curve.py (8 cities, WGS84 distances, no ellipse comparison)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No pipeline computes the 48.6% RMS reduction. Visualization scripts hardcode ellipse parameters; find_curve.py has no elliptical correction; no circular-vs-elliptical comparison exists. The c2 dome_code_analysis remains accurate as of c3 — no new code changes affect this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: All ellipse parameters hardcoded (a=20015, b=15000, orient=60°). The b/a 0.70–0.90 range is asserted in descriptive text without a convergence script. The 48.6% RMS figure exists only in prose.
data_sources_used: WGS84 reference distances between 8 city pairs (hardcoded in find_curve.py)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-003 ('Ellipse parameters still converging') is an indefinite escape — the ellipse can be retuned whenever errors are found, and the dome's own OPEN-001 admits dome-native coordinates haven't been derived.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The c3 detail_evidence correctly characterizes the visualization-only role of the ellipse parameters and the absence of an RMS computation script.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain or fetch logic for elliptical-disc geometry. The 8-city distance evaluation in find_curve.py runs on script-invocation only, not as part of the 5-minute monitoring loop. Confirmed by direct grep over raw-text/monitor.py — no 'ellipse', 'disc_shape', or 'elliptic' references.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard cartography (Snyder, USGS Professional Paper 1395) explains the latitude-dependent radial distance distortion of the polar azimuthal equidistant projection of a sphere. The dome relabels this distortion 'the disc is elliptical' while continuing to fit globe-derived inter-city distances.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: All inter-city distances were established by 20th-century geodesy (radar, laser-ranging, GPS, VLBI) decades before the dome model fit them. The 'prediction' is the fit residual, not the distances themselves.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: a, b, orient are empirical curve-fit outputs against WGS84 distances. No derivation from disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, or any other dome-specific parameter produces b/a≈0.75 or orient≈60°. Across V5–V9 the values drift, which is characteristic of fitting, not derivation.

holes_found.description: STALE CROSS-REFERENCE. The detail_verdict_text reads 'see Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b', but no anchor or section labeled 'Kill-Shot #1' exists in the current site. Part 5 (Kill Shots) contains exactly one numbered subsection (5.0 — Author-Initiated Demotions) and one named killshot analysis (the dome's flat-disc geodesy claim — h3s 'The dome's claim' / 'The numeric chain' / 'The aetheric refraction escape hatch' / 'Independent confirmation: New Horizons' / 'The kernel of truth'). The kill-shots are not numbered #1, #2, #3 — the V6 restructure (Part 3.3 → Part 5) extracted a single killshot writeup, not a numbered set. Readers who click through expecting a 'Kill-Shot #1' will find no such anchor. A dome defender will use this exact pattern as ammunition: 'The critic complains our parameters are inconsistent across versions, but their own internal cross-references point to nonexistent sections.' The Part 2b reference resolves to section_key part2b correctly; only 'Kill-Shot #1' is broken. Verified by JSON-grep over data/sections.json against patterns matching 'Kill-Shot' followed by digits — zero matches in any section body.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'see Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b' with 'see the Kill-Shot section (Part 5) and Part 2b' — or, if the intent was the flat-disc-geodesy killshot specifically, cite it by topic: 'see the flat-disc geodesy Kill-Shot in Part 5 and Part 2b'. Both phrasings remove the broken numeric reference while preserving the rhetorical weight.

holes_found.description: C2 STRONGER_ARGUMENT NOT INTEGRATED — 'nested models always nest.' The Cycle 2 review (2026-04-07) explicitly recommended adding a sentence about nested-model triviality: an ellipse with free b/a subsumes the circle (b/a=1) as a special case, so 'ellipse never lost to circle across V5–V9' is mathematically guaranteed and rhetorically vacuous. After ~25 days and one substantive rewrite, this argument still does not appear in detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text. It would preempt any defender who cites 'never reversed across versions' as evidence of the ellipse being a real discovery.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence after the AIC/BIC clause: 'Note that the ellipse-vs-circle comparison is structurally a nested-model test: setting b/a = 1 collapses the ellipse to a circle, so an ellipse fit can never produce a worse RMS than a circle fit on the same data. The dome's "ellipse never lost" framing across V5–V9 is mathematically guaranteed, not empirically informative.'

holes_found.description: WGS84-CIRCULARITY PREEMPTION MISSING (Lens A, defense rated 3/5). The detail_evidence asserts 'the inter-city distances in this dataset are computed from WGS84 globe coordinates, so the ellipse is not discovering the Earth's shape — it is absorbing the radial scaling distortion.' A capable dome defender will counter: 'You've assumed WGS84 in order to debunk us — that's begging the question.' Our text doesn't preempt this. The defense is not trivially refuted because we never explicitly point out that inter-city distance is independently confirmed by multiple metrology methods that don't presuppose WGS84 (radar ranging, GPS time-of-flight, fiber-optic latency, VLBI baseline interferometry — and the dome model itself uses GPS-derived satellite tracking via OpenSky in monitor.py). The defender's circularity charge collapses once the convergence-of-independent-methods argument is on the page.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence after the 'WGS84-derived distances' clause: 'A defender may reply that we have presupposed WGS84 in our critique, but inter-city distances are independently confirmed by radar ranging, GPS time-of-flight, fiber-optic latency, and VLBI baseline interferometry — methods that do not require a sphere assumption and converge on the same values. The dome model's own monitoring scripts (e.g., OpenSky satellite tracking in monitor.py) consume GPS-derived data, so the model cannot consistently use GPS while disputing the distances GPS produces.'

holes_found.description: RHETORICAL WEAKNESS — the detail_verdict_text repeats the post-hoc test boilerplate ('The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters...') verbatim across many WINs. This is fine for cross-WIN consistency but in WIN-030 specifically the boilerplate slightly mischaracterizes the failure mode: WIN-030 is not 'dome derives a value, then matches it to globe-measured value' — it is 'dome fits free parameters to globe-measured values, then claims the resulting fit as a discovery.' The post-hoc charge holds either way, but the fitting-vs-derivation distinction matters for accuracy.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding one specific phrase to the post-hoc paragraph: 'In WIN-030 specifically, the "derivation" is itself a curve fit — a, b, orient are tuned to minimize residuals against the very globe-distance dataset they are then claimed to validate.' Low priority — the boilerplate is functional, this is sharpening.

stronger_arguments.description: GPS metrology preemption — see hole #3 above. Adding the multi-method convergence argument neutralizes the strongest dome defense.
sources: Misra & Enge (2011), Global Positioning System: Signals, Measurements, and Performance, 2nd ed. — independent confirmation of WGS84 baselines via GPS
Petit & Luzum eds. (2010), IERS Conventions, IERS TN 36 — VLBI baseline solutions
raw-text/monitor.py — dome's own use of OpenSky (GPS-derived satellite positions)

stronger_arguments.description: Nested-model triviality — see hole #2 above. C2 carryover.
sources: Burnham & Anderson (2002), Model Selection and Multimodel Inference, Springer — Sections 1.2–2.5 on nested models
Akaike (1974), 'A new look at the statistical model identification', IEEE TAC 19(6):716–723

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model has correctly identified that distances measured on the surface of a sphere, when forced onto a flat azimuthal-equidistant projection, exhibit systematic latitude-dependent error — and that the residual structure has a roughly elliptical shape. This is real cartographic phenomenology (data/wins.json:030 detail_evidence — 'radial scaling distortion that the Azimuthal Equidistant projection introduces'). The same structure is independently flagged in WIN-027 (data/wins.json:027 — '0.00131·lat² + 0.06828·lat + 1.06719... the numerical signature of a spherical surface being forced onto a plane') and WIN-058 (data/wins.json:058 — 'accuracy 7.3% is 600–730× worse than Vincenty geodesy'), so the observation reproduces across multiple dome WINs and is internally consistent in our review.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Detecting projection distortion on globe-derived data is exactly what spherical geometry predicts — the dome's ellipse is the model inadvertently measuring globe curvature, not disc shape. Each new dome version that 'improves' the ellipse (data/wins.json:030 detail_evidence — 'b/a wanders from 0.70 to 0.90') is the dome adding free parameters to better approximate what spherical trigonometry handles with zero free parameters via Vincenty (raw-text/find_curve.py — 'WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001)' as cited in WIN-058). The WGS84 b/a value of 0.997 (verified this run: 6356752.3142 / 6378137 = 0.996647) is reached from first principles by fitting a planet's rotational figure; the dome's b/a wanders by 29% across versions because there is no first-principle anchor. Convergence vs. drift is the empirical signature that distinguishes measurement from fitting.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender who has read our methodological principles can mount a three-pronged defense. First, on AIC/BIC: 'The critic invokes AIC/BIC as a generic objection without computing it. With 8 city pairs and 2 added parameters, the AIC penalty is 2k = 4 log-likelihood units. The dome reports 48.6% RMS reduction — show the chi-squared and demonstrate the penalty exceeds the improvement, or your AIC objection is just hand-waving.' Second, on WGS84 circularity: 'Inter-city distances are computed from WGS84, which is the globe model we're testing against. To use WGS84 distances as ground truth in a flat-Earth/dome critique is to assume your conclusion. Our OPEN-001 explicitly acknowledges that dome-native coordinates remain an open research item — that is intellectual honesty, not concession.' Third, on b/a wandering: 'Newton's gravitational constant changed by ~10% across the first decade of measurement. Iterative scientific refinement of a parameter from 0.70 to 0.90 over five versions reflects normal model maturation. The critic punishes both transparency (publishing OPEN-003) AND uncertainty — there is no acceptable behavior here.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add the GPS-metrology / multi-method convergence sentence (see hole #3) to neutralize the WGS84-circularity prong, which is the strongest of the three. The AIC/BIC and b/a-wandering prongs collapse on inspection (AIC penalty 2k = 4 vs. claimed 48.6% RMS reduction — but without a baseline RMS the comparison is impossible from our side AND from the dome's side, which is exactly our point; the b/a 29% drift is two orders of magnitude larger than typical scientific parameter refinement and the dome itself flags OPEN-003 as unresolved). The circularity charge, by contrast, requires a positive rebuttal from us — silence is cost. The recommended preemption (independent metrology methods + the dome's own GPS dependency) defuses it cleanly without expanding the WIN beyond TLDR-friendly length.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-027 (Southern distance quadratic, Misleading) makes the same projection-distortion argument with a different dataset (quadratic ratio coefficients 0.00131·lat² + 0.06828·lat + 1.06719). WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate θ = -lonE, Misleading) cites the same OPEN-001 (WGS-84 dependency persists) and the same accuracy gap (7.3% vs Vincenty geodesy). WIN-031 (North Pole cosmic mountain, Unfalsifiable) shares the cartographic-fit pattern but applies it to mythopoetic geography rather than measured distances. All three reinforce WIN-030's critique: the dome's coordinate system is curve-fitting to globe data, not deriving from disc geometry. No defender could compile a 'your review contradicts itself' attack across these four WINs — they are mutually reinforcing.

claims_checked: 48.6% RMS reduction figure (asserted unverifiable in detail_evidence — verified absence of computation)
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) Δ(b/a) variation: (0.90 - 0.70) / 0.70 = 0.2857 = 28.57%, rounds to 29% as claimed. Note: an alternative denominator choice (midpoint 0.80) yields 25%, and high-end (0.90) yields 22%. The text uses the lowest denominator, which is the largest figure but defensible since the dome's reported V5 value is in fact b/a≈0.70. (2) WGS84 axes a = 6378137.0 m, b = 6356752.3142 m (NIMA TR8350.2 / WGS84 reference); b/a = 0.996647, which rounds to 0.997 to three decimals. The detail_evidence rounding is correct. (3) Searched the dome repo (raw-text/monitor.py, raw-text/pull_data.py, find_curve.py, generate_advanced_map_v2.py, test_curve_stretching.py) for any script that computes a circular-vs-elliptical RMS comparison or outputs '48.6': zero matches. The unverifiability claim holds.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 review of WIN-030. The cycle 2 review (2026-04-07) flagged two major holes (duplicated phrase in finding; redundant WGS84 paragraphs in detail_evidence) and one moderate (missing b/a wandering point). The duplicated phrase is fixed; the redundant paragraphs are consolidated; the b/a 29% point is now in detail_evidence with the WGS84 b/a = 0.997 comparison and the OPEN-003 self-acknowledgment. Recommended_action c2 = minor_edit. The c2 minor edits were applied — that's good progress.

However, c3 surfaces three new findings:

1) MAJOR — stale 'Kill-Shot #1' cross-reference. The V6 restructure (2026-04-07) extracted Part 3.3 → Part 5 (Kill Shots), but the current Part 5 has no '#1' anchor or numbered subsection. There is one named killshot writeup (the flat-disc geodesy / Hawaii distance argument) and one update section (5.0 author demotions). This reference was likely stale before the restructure and remained stale through it; nothing in c2 caught it because c2 was written immediately before/around the restructure (2026-04-07) and didn't audit cross-references. Reader-impact: a defender or skeptical reader who clicks through 'Kill-Shot #1' lands on Part 5 and finds no #1 — instant credibility hit.

2) MODERATE — c2 stronger_argument about nested-model triviality still not in text. C2 explicitly recommended adding a sentence preempting the 'ellipse never lost to circle across V5-V9' rhetoric. Twenty-five days and one rewrite later, the sentence still isn't there. This is a recurring pattern worth flagging in a tracker — c2 stronger_arguments are advisory, but when they're as load-bearing as the nested-model point, they should percolate into edits.

3) MODERATE — Lens A surfaced a defense rated 3/5: WGS84-circularity. Our text invokes WGS84 distances as ground truth without preempting the obvious 'you're begging the question' counter. The fix is one sentence about multi-method metrology convergence (radar, GPS, VLBI, fiber-optic) plus the dome's own GPS dependency via OpenSky. This is rhetorically important because a charitable defender CAN make this charge with some force, and our cost of preempting it is one sentence.

Lens B (cross-WIN consistency) passes cleanly: WIN-027, WIN-058, WIN-031 all reinforce WIN-030's critique with no contradictions, no source-citation drift. The 95.2%/94.5% accuracy reference in detail_evidence resolves correctly to Section 6.6 in part6 (verified — the 95.2 figure appears in part1, part2b, part4, part6, part8 with consistent characterization). The Part 2b cross-reference resolves to section_key part2b cleanly. Only 'Kill-Shot #1' is broken.

Lens C (quantitative verification) confirms: the 29% variation claim is correct under the dome's own version-trajectory framing (28.57% rounded), and the WGS84 b/a = 0.997 is correct (true value 0.99664718 rounds to 0.997). The 48.6% RMS unverifiability claim is empirically supported — no script in the dome repo computes a circular-vs-elliptical RMS comparison.

The 'Misleading' verdict still holds with high confidence (0.88 retained). Recommended action: minor_edit to fix the stale Kill-Shot reference and add the two preemptive sentences (nested-model triviality + GPS metrology convergence). No verdict change indicated.

Finally, on the user's preference for considering the other side: the dome author's strongest available counter is the WGS84-circularity charge, and it is genuinely worth taking seriously — a flat-disc model that needs to test its predictions against a globe-derived metric is in a bind. The reason the charge fails is not that WGS84 is unimpeachable but that the dome model itself runs on GPS data (OpenSky satellite ephemerides) and so cannot coherently dispute the metric. This is a stronger response than 'WGS84 is right because spherical geometry,' and the recommended hole #3 patch encodes it.
